recent_coach_change: No change. Deschamps has announced the 2026 World Cup will be his final tournament in charge before stepping down.
qualification_path: Qualified through UEFA qualifying as group winners.
key_players: Kylian Mbappe (Real Madrid) — captain and main attacking force; needs two goals to break France's World Cup scoring record
Ousmane Dembele (Paris Saint-Germain) — reigning Ballon d'Or winner and Champions League title holder
William Saliba (Arsenal) — anchor of the back line
Michael Olise (Bayern Munich) — strong club season, key attacking option

injuries_doubts.issue: Various niggles/injuries through the season; managed minutes in warm-ups

squad_notes: Eduardo Camavinga left out after a difficult, injury-affected season with reduced minutes at Real Madrid. Randal Kolo Muani also missed the cut. Squad still regarded as the most richly talented pool in world football.
warmup_assessment: Mixed. France were stunned 2-1 by Ivory Coast in Nantes on 4 June 2026 in their final warm-up; Rayan Cherki opened the scoring but a heavily rotated second-half side conceded twice, raising questions about depth and Deschamps' tactical approach.
shape_verdict: Loaded with talent and among the tournament favourites, but the late friendly defeat to Ivory Coast exposed vulnerability when rotating and questions persist over whether Deschamps maximises the squad.

news.headline: France stunned 2-1 by Ivory Coast in World Cup warm-up defeat
France World Cup squad named; Camavinga and Kolo Muani miss out
France World Cup 2026 preview: among the favourites under outgoing Deschamps
